scenario_results.reason: The lane resolved a standard beauty recovery case only when the booking, formula, guest-history, and delegated redo records aligned.
Missing client, visit, provider, and product prerequisites produced a blocked-state response instead of speculative retention handling.
Unsupported package restoration, fee waiver, and loyalty exceptions were treated as approval-bound escalations and not executed in-lane.
A retention conversation that turned into treatment-suitability and med-spa guidance was handed to the correct licensed or clinical owner.
Repeat service-recovery failures and cross-location record conflicts triggered an escalation package instead of another generic apology.
Product-quality, infection, and licensure adjudication requests were routed to specialized adjacent owners rather than over-resolved by the retention lane.
The lane rejected generic CRM framing and re-established beauty-specific guest-history, consultation, provider, and package evidence requirements.
Conflicting public review and recurring-cancellation guidance versus tenant scripts triggered escalation rather than false-confidence synthesis.
Unsupported package-transfer and promotional workaround requests were rejected cleanly and treated as boundary-sensitive policy issues.
Tenant-specific patch-test, minors, and approval overlays were applied as stricter constraints without losing the public baseline.

The in-scope scenario preserved beauty terminology such as guest history card, formula card, toner refresh, lash fill, brow lamination, and treatment room rebook instead of collapsing into generic support wording.
Missing provider, patch-test, product-lot, and harm-severity facts triggered low-confidence escalation instead of confident synthesis.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the service-support-and-resolution and cross-industry contract.

The package stays inside beauty retention, complaint recovery, and escalation packaging and does not claim refund-override, review-manipulation, product-defect adjudication, legal interpretation, or clinical authority.
